data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 42c2-39e1-16cd-455c (CACHE-INTEG), child 7a41-7cc3-62f5-4efa "child one"] This child's Acceptance Criterion ("a"), Scope ("x"), Why ("a") and What ("first child") are opaque placeholders that make no mention of cache, container fan-out, or reads, and do not map to either parent criterion. Cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster: sibling 6019-d533-79a0-4017 is also placeholder-only ("b"/"y"), so neither parent criterion is covered by any child. 4-bucket result: Parent success "container calls cache." = UNCOVERED; Parent acceptance "cache reads on later container pairings" = UNCOVERED. See findings below.

FINDINGS:

Finding 1 — Parent success criterion "container calls cache." is uncovered by the union of children.
- criteria: ["Parent success: container calls cache."]
- location: parent 42c2-39e1-16cd-455c Success Criteria; child 7a41-7cc3-62f5-4efa Acceptance/Scope
- evidence: ["Child AC = 'a', Scope = 'x', What = 'first child' — no reference to cache or container call", "Sibling 6019-d533-79a0-4017 AC = 'b', Scope = 'y' — equally placeholder, no cache/container reference"]
- scenarios: ["Children are implemented to their stated ACs ('a' and 'b') yet no child verifies that the container actually calls cache, leaving the parent success criterion unmet at rollup."]
- impact: Parent's sole success criterion has no child carrying it; the epic could close green while the integration behavior it exists to prove is never exercised.
- checklist_item: The union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion — 4-bucket audit; an uncovered criterion is a finding.
- suggested_fix: Make one child explicitly cover "container calls cache" (e.g., rewrite child one's AC/Scope to assert the container fan-out invokes cache.), or add a child that does.

Finding 2 — Parent acceptance criterion "cache reads on later container pairings" is uncovered by the union of children.
- criteria: ["Parent acceptance: cache reads on later container pairings"]
- location: parent 42c2-39e1-16cd-455c Acceptance Criteria; child 7a41-7cc3-62f5-4efa Acceptance/Scope
- evidence: ["Child AC = 'a', Scope = 'x' — no reference to cache reads or later pairings", "Sibling 6019-d533-79a0-4017 AC = 'b', Scope = 'y' — no reference to cache reads or later pairings"]
- scenarios: ["Both children pass their placeholder ACs but nothing asserts that later container pairings read from cache, so the integration's caching guarantee is unverified."]
- impact: The acceptance criterion that distinguishes the integration test (read-on-later-pairing) is carried by no child.
- checklist_item: The union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion — 4-bucket audit; an uncovered criterion is a finding.
- suggested_fix: Assign coverage of "cache reads on later container pairings" to a child (rewrite child one or child two's AC/Scope to assert a cache read occurs on subsequent container pairings).
